prompt: Design an inverting amplifier using an ideal op-amp built from a voltage-controlled voltage source (VCVS). The amplifier should invert and amplify the input signal Vin and present the result at Vout, using resistors to set the closed-loop gain.
reference_netlist:
Inverting Amplifier (Level 1 compile-only)
* Ports: Vin (input), Vout (output)

* Power rail (kept for convention)
Vdd vdd 0 DC 5.0

* Input source
Vin vin 0 DC 0 AC 1

* Resistor network around the inverting node (vn)
Rin  vin vn   1k
Rf   vn  vout 10k

* Non-inverting input grounded
Rg   vp  0    1k

* Ideal op-amp modeled as a high-gain VCVS: Vout = A*(vp - vn)
Eopamp vout 0 vp vn 1e6

.control
op
print v(vout)
.endc

.end

prompt: Design an inverting amplifier with input node Vin and output node Vout, powered by ±5 V supplies (vdd=+5V, vss=-5V) with the non-inverting input of the active device referenced to ground. The closed-loop DC voltage gain magnitude must lie between 19.5 dB and 20.5 dB (nominal -10 V/V, i.e. -20 dB). The -3 dB small-signal closed-loop bandwidth must be at least 500 kHz. A behavioral single-pole opamp macromodel is acceptable; the netlist must expose gain and bandwidth via .measure directives.
reference_netlist:
* Inverting Amplifier L2 reference (Av=-10 V/V, BW>=500 kHz)
Vdd vdd 0 DC 5
Vss vss 0 DC -5
Vin vin 0 AC 1 DC 0

R1 vin vn 1k
Rf vn vout 10k

* Behavioral op-amp: A0=1e5 (100 dB), dominant pole at 100 Hz -> GBW = 10 MHz
* Closed-loop gain = -Rf/R1 = -10 (20 dB); closed-loop BW = GBW/(1+Rf/R1) ~= 909 kHz
Eopen vint 0 0 vn 1e5
Rp vint vp 1k
Cp vp 0 1.5915uF
Ebuf vout 0 vp 0 1

.ac dec 20 1 100Meg
.measure ac av_db FIND vdb(vout) AT=10
.measure ac av_db_max MAX vdb(vout)
.measure ac bw_hz WHEN vdb(vout)=17 FALL=1

.end

prompt: Design a single-supply inverting amplifier (input port Vin, output port Vout) with a nominal closed-loop voltage gain of -10 V/V (20 dB) at 1 kHz that maintains gain accuracy across five PVT corners. The amplifier is AC-coupled at the input and biased from a resistor divider so that the inverting input sits at VDD/2 under quiescent conditions. The five corners to evaluate are: C1 nominal (VDD=5.0 V, T=27 °C), C2 (VDD=4.5 V, T=0 °C), C3 (VDD=5.5 V, T=0 °C), C4 (VDD=4.5 V, T=70 °C), and C5 (VDD=5.5 V, T=70 °C). The figure-of-merit is the AC gain magnitude at 1 kHz expressed in dB, which must lie within [19.5 dB, 20.5 dB] (i.e. closed-loop gain magnitude 9.44–10.59 V/V, ±5%) at each corner. The reference implementation uses a high-gain behavioral op-amp (VCVS, A=1e5) wrapped in resistive feedback (R1=1 kΩ from Vin, R2=10 kΩ in feedback) and sweeps the corners inside a `.control` block via `alter @Vdd[dc]` and `set temp`. Each corner runs an `ac dec 20 1 100k` analysis followed by `.meas ac gain_cN find vdb(vout) at=1000` so all five measured gains appear in stdout. The circuit is judged passing if at least 4 of 5 corners meet the gain window (≥ 80 % corner yield).
reference_netlist:
* Inverting amplifier - PVT corner sweep (L3 composite)
.title InvertingAmp_PVT

Vdd vdd 0 dc 5
Vin vin_src 0 ac 1 dc 0

* AC coupling so DC bias is set by feedback only
Cin vin_src vin 100u

* Mid-supply bias for non-inverting input (tracks VDD/2)
Rb1 vdd vplus 10k
Rb2 vplus 0 10k
Cb vplus 0 1u

* Inverting amp: closed-loop gain = -R2/R1 = -10
R1 vin vminus 1k
R2 vminus vout 10k

* Behavioral op-amp: high open-loop gain VCVS
Eopamp vout 0 vplus vminus 1e5

.control
echo "=== Corner 1: VDD=5.0 T=27 (nominal) ==="
alter @Vdd[dc] = 5.0
set temp = 27
ac dec 20 1 100k
meas ac gain_c1 find vdb(vout) at=1000

echo "=== Corner 2: VDD=4.5 T=0 ==="
alter @Vdd[dc] = 4.5
set temp = 0
ac dec 20 1 100k
meas ac gain_c2 find vdb(vout) at=1000

echo "=== Corner 3: VDD=5.5 T=0 ==="
alter @Vdd[dc] = 5.5
set temp = 0
ac dec 20 1 100k
meas ac gain_c3 find vdb(vout) at=1000

echo "=== Corner 4: VDD=4.5 T=70 ==="
alter @Vdd[dc] = 4.5
set temp = 70
ac dec 20 1 100k
meas ac gain_c4 find vdb(vout) at=1000

echo "=== Corner 5: VDD=5.5 T=70 ==="
alter @Vdd[dc] = 5.5
set temp = 70
ac dec 20 1 100k
meas ac gain_c5 find vdb(vout) at=1000

quit
.endc

.end
